channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Incredibly concerned': Zero dog fighting-related charges laid in years
item.description: Experts say dog fighting is going under the radar in Queensland following administrative changes to the state's Animal Care and Protection Act.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: A dog fighting and cock fighting unit was formed in 2017 and sat within RSPCA Queensland, staffed by three officers.

item.title: Icebreaker's costly refuelling detour could take years to fix
item.description: A costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker might not be resolved for another three years under most options, internal government documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Front on view of RSV Nuyina, AAD research and supply icebreaker ship.

item.title: An 'outside the square' Bledisloe Cup idea can be a win for Aussie rugby
item.description: Australian rugby lacks a marquee event that captures the national imagination each year, but this could change if a bold Bledisloe Cup proposal gets approval.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: The Wallabies and All Blacks could face each other on Anzac Day in the coming years.

item.title: 'Do or die': Flick's journey from jail to artist and First Nations mentor
item.description: A day after her 18th birthday, Flick Chafer-Smith hit rock bottom when she was sent to jail. She found salvation through art.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Ferrier

media:group.media:description: Flick Chafer-Smith spent six years in and out of jail before finding a different pathway through art.

item.title: $100m plan to use AI to speed up housing
item.description: Artificial intelligence will be recruited to speed up assessments of housing and energy projects waiting for environmental approval, as the government races to build more homes and ward off a key frustration turning voters away from the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Housing reforms are tipped to be a key measure of the federal budget.

item.title: Shell game: How Australian gas giants are using Singapore to reduce taxes
item.description: Amid renewed debate over whether Australia is getting enough tax from its gas exports, an investigation by the ABC shows how resource giants use shell companies to shift profit.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mercer and Neil Chenoweth

item.title: Australian plane gave NATO 'invaluable' boost during Russian incursion
item.description: A European official says they'd be "very happy" if Australia deployed the Wedgetail aircraft again, after it played an important role as NATO responded to an incursion of Russian jets over Estonia.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Australia's E-7A Wedgetail aircraft have a radar with a range of 400 kilometres.

item.title: Inside the course targeting young tradies with get-rich schemes
item.description: "Escape the 9-5" content is blowing up on social media, but people who have gotten caught up in it warn of the costs.
item.dc:creator: Alice Angeloni

media:group.media:description: Billy* is an apprentice carpenter from Western Australia who signed up for ABC Academy.

item.title: Embassy break-in exposes China-Japan trust at 'historic low'
item.description: An incident involving a Japanese soldier allegedly scaling the wall of China's embassy in Tokyo a month ago has become a lightning rod in a tension-riddled landscape between China and Japan.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Whiting

media:group.media:description: Japanese police arrested a local military officer on suspicion of trespass after an alleged break-in at China's embassy in Tokyo.

item.title: 'They're people's mums and dads': The crisis engulfing hospitals
item.description: The number of aged care patients stuck in hospitals has jumped by 35 per cent in less than a year, fuelling fresh demands for the federal government to stem the crisis.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Dalzell, Paige Cockburn, and Rhiannon Hobbins

media:group.media:description: Irene (left) was medically cleared to leave hospital, but had to stay longer because she couldn't access appropriate home support.

item.title: One Nation didn't just win. The Liberal vote collapsed spectacularly
item.description: Drafting Barnaby Joyce created a permission structure that has allowed conservative or swing voters to support One Nation: if the bloke who was deputy prime minister thinks the Coalition has lost its way, something in the system must be broken.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Karvelas

media:group.media:description: After Saturday's win in Farrer, Barnaby Joyce will have a seat-mate in the lower house.

item.title: The federal budget is tomorrow. Here's why it matters
item.description: Budget night features a lot of political talk and policy wonkiness, but the government's plan will impact your financial situation in one way or another, so it's quite important. Here's what you need to know.

media:group.media:description: This year's federal budget is shaping up to be one of the biggest in decades.

item.title: The government has missed one idea to get housing back on track
item.description: If it's serious about improving intergenerational fairness and housing affordability in this week's budget, perhaps the federal government should just build some houses.
item.dc:creator: Alan Kohler

media:group.media:description: Houses and apartments cost between $350,000 and $400,000 each to build.

item.title: Fashion designers are experimenting with AI, but not everyone is a fan
item.description: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Jane Rocca

media:group.media:description: Melbourne-based designer Karla Špetić, whose works are seen here at this year's Melbourne Fashion Festival, had mixed results using AI to create clothing.

item.title: Quiz: How much did Michael Jordan's signed Air Nike 1s sell for in 2020?
item.description: From the Tudor period right through to the 21st century, test your knowledge of the past with this week's history quiz.

media:group.media:description: A pair of basketball legend Michael Jordan's famous Air Jordans from his rookie season.

item.title: 'We were weird': Tim Finn on Split Enz's rise
item.description: Tim Finn on the past, the present and the possible future of Split Enz.
item.dc:creator: Dan Condon

media:group.media:description: Split Enz's latest line-up features Neil and Tim Finn, Eddie Rayner, Noel Crombie, James Milne and Matt Eccles.

item.title: Jay Vine escapes serious injury after horrifying crash at Giro d'Italia
item.description: Australian cyclist Jay Vine has sustained a fractured elbow and concussion after suffering his third crash in 13 race days this year.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jay Vine is out of the Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Phoenix makes A-League history with semifinal triumph over Brisbane
item.description: Wellington Phoenix has become the first side from New Zealand to make an A-League grand final after inching past Brisbane Roar in their semifinal.

media:group.media:description: Woods's double fired Wellington into the grand final.

item.title: Netball body suspends woman charged over alleged antisemitic slur at game
item.description: Jewish community leaders say a woman's alleged slur at an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney reflects a troubling pattern playing out on sporting fields.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Pengilley

media:group.media:description: A woman is confronted after allegedly making antisemitic comments during an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney's east.

item.title: Fuel injection as SA announces $40m diesel stockpile
item.description: A strategic diesel reserve of between 10 million and 20 million litres will be established in SA to bolster the supply and provide assurance to farmers, the state government says.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Keane

media:group.media:description: A strategic diesel reserve has been announced for SA in order to boost supplies amid ongoing uncertainty among farmers.

item.title: When Nicole started checking her breasts, she never expected to find a lump
item.description: Breast cancer survivor Nicole Speirs is among thousands of people who turned out for Mother's Day Classic charity fun run events across the country.
item.dc:creator: Penny Travers and Ruby Thomas

media:group.media:description: Nicole Speirs was diagnosed with breast cancer 18 months ago.

item.title: Sabalenka exits Rome with injury concern ahead of French Open
item.description: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Aryna Sabalenka says she has a lower back and hip problem ahead of the French Open, after her third round loss in Rome.

item.title: Premier backs away from revealing reasons for backbencher's legal costs
item.description: Tasmania's premier is under fire for backtracking on an undertaking to explain why taxpayers paid a $15,000 legal bill for a backbencher.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Jaggers

media:group.media:description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff with Liberal Member for Lyons Mark Shelton in 2025.

item.title: Man, 18, dies after falling from sixth-floor balcony while fleeing police
item.description: Police say the young man was attempting to escape over the balcony when he fell and sustained critical injuries.

media:group.media:description: Police say the man attempted to flee over a sixth-floor balcony before falling.

item.title: Sunwater chair labels costly dam rebuild 'grossly disproportionate'
item.description: In a letter addressed to the water minister, Sunwater chair Jeff Seeney said the decision to rebuild the dam lacked "any rigour" or "professional credibility".
item.dc:creator: Stephen Clarke and Emma Johnson

media:group.media:description: Sunwater chair Jeff Seeney has written to the Queensland government recommending against rebuilding the troubled Paradise Dam.
